quickAnswer.heading: The short answer
items: Weed barrier fabric is the simplest greenhouse flooring: clean, draining, low-maintenance
It blocks sunlight to weeds while letting water and air through
A woven fabric like SHIELD 3.2oz lays flat and lasts for years
Prep, measure, overlap seams, and anchor for a floor that stays put
For heavier benches and cart traffic, step up to XBAR 5oz

blocks.heading: Greenhouse flooring made simple
blocks.body: Building a greenhouse extends your growing season and creates a controlled environment, and one essential element is the greenhouse flooring, where weed fabric plays a critical role. Weeds compete with your plants for nutrients, water, and sunlight, and in the warm, moist conditions of a greenhouse they flourish fast. A woven weed barrier creates a clean foundation that blocks weeds while letting water drain, which keeps the space tidy and your plants healthier. Among the greenhouse weed control options, fabric barriers are among the most effective and the easiest to install. Size your floor with the [DOTDAY Fabric Calculator](https://www.thedotday.com/landscape-fabric-calculator).

blocks.heading: Why weed barrier fabric works for greenhouse floors
blocks.body: Weed fabric gives a greenhouse floor a few clear advantages. It manages moisture by letting water penetrate while reducing excessive evaporation, helping keep soil moisture consistent. It adds a layer of separation that helps regulate the surface and keeps mud and splash-up off your pots and walkways. And it cuts weeding dramatically: by blocking the sunlight weed seeds need to germinate, a woven barrier means far less time pulling weeds and more time tending plants. Choosing a quality woven fabric like [SHIELD](https://www.thedotday.com/product-page/dotday-shield-weed-barrier-fabric-heavy-duty-3-2oz-landscape-fabric) 3.2oz ensures long-term suppression and a cleaner growing space.

blocks.alt: DOTDAY SHIELD woven fabric being stretched, the durable choice for greenhouse flooring
blocks.caption: A durable woven fabric lays flat and holds up to constant watering and foot traffic.

blocks.heading: How to install weed fabric in a greenhouse

steps.title: Prepare the ground
steps.body: Clear the greenhouse area of all vegetation and debris so the fabric lays flat and functions well.

steps.title: Measure and cut
steps.body: Measure the floor area and cut the fabric to fit, leaving a little excess at the edges for anchoring.

steps.title: Lay the fabric
steps.body: Place the fabric directly on the prepared ground with a slight overlap at the seams to close any gaps.

steps.title: Secure it
steps.body: Use landscape fabric anchors or stakes to hold the fabric in place so it does not shift during watering.

steps.title: Add your surface
steps.body: Top with gravel or pavers for walkways if you want a finished floor, keeping the fabric covered and protected.

blocks.heading: Flooring multiple greenhouses or grow areas?
blocks.body: DOTDAY supplies SHIELD to growers and nurseries direct. Request bulk and pro pricing for large production areas.

blocks.heading: Pick the right weight for the traffic
blocks.body: For a standard greenhouse floor with foot traffic and pots, [SHIELD](https://www.thedotday.com/product-page/dotday-shield-weed-barrier-fabric-heavy-duty-3-2oz-landscape-fabric) 3.2oz woven is ideal: breathable, durable, and easy to cut. If you run heavy benches, loaded carts, or a gravel walkway with real load, step up to [XBAR](https://www.thedotday.com/product-page/dotday-xbar-weed-barrier-fabric-heavy-duty-5oz-dual-layer) 5oz so the fabric does not tear. To compare how the weights hold up over time, read [what landscape fabric lasts the longest](https://www.thedotday.com/post/what-landscape-fabric-lasts-the-longest).

blocks.heading: Want help speccing your greenhouse floor?
blocks.body: Tell the DOTDAY team your greenhouse size and how you use the space, and we will recommend the right fabric and quantity.

items.q: What flooring works best in a greenhouse?
items.a: For most home and production greenhouses, a woven weed barrier fabric over a cleared, leveled base is the simplest effective floor. It blocks weeds, lets water drain, and keeps the space clean and low-maintenance. A 3.2oz woven fabric like SHIELD lays flat and lasts for years, and you can top it with gravel or pavers for finished walkways where you need firmer footing.

items.q: Does a greenhouse floor need to drain?
items.a: Yes. Constant watering means a greenhouse floor has to let water pass through rather than pool, which is exactly what a permeable woven weed fabric does. It drains while still blocking weeds from below and keeping soil and mud off your pots and paths. Lay it over leveled ground and, if you want a firmer surface, add a gravel layer on top that also drains freely.

items.q: Can you put weed fabric directly on greenhouse soil?
items.a: Yes. Lay a woven weed barrier directly on cleared, leveled greenhouse soil, overlapping seams slightly and anchoring it so it stays put during watering. The fabric blocks sunlight to weed seeds while letting moisture through. For a more finished floor, add gravel or pavers on top for walkways, which protects the fabric and gives you clean, firm footing between beds and benches.
